task_completion.description: GIMP is open with the image loaded in the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible with a landscape image displayed on the canvas.
The 'Scale Image' dialog (accessed via Image menu) or the 'Scale' tool dialog is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The width is set to 800 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scaling dialog, the 'Width' input field contains the value '800'.

task_completion.description: The aspect ratio is maintained during scaling.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chain link icon next to the Width and Height fields is linked (unbroken), and/or the 'Height' input field automatically shows approximately '533' pixels.

task_completion.description: The scaling operation is applied to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scaling dialog is closed, the image appears smaller on the canvas, and/or the GIMP title bar or status bar reflects the new dimensions (800x533).

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical interface to scale the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GIMP application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GIMP interface is visible, not a mock application or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripting consoles to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python-fu or Script-fu consoles are not used to execute the scaling command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original image is 1200x800 pixels (3:2 aspect ratio). The target width is exactly 800 pixels. To maintain the aspect ratio, the height must automatically adjust to approximately 533 pixels. The VLM should look for the width being set to 800 and the height to ~533 in the GIMP scaling dialog.
